title: Canadian Nosocomial Infection Surveillance Program (CNISP) - Canada.ca
content:
## Canadian Nosocomial Infection Surveillance Program (CNISP)
- Surveillance data publications
The Canadian Nosocomial Infection Surveillance Program (CNISP) is a sentinel hospital network that includes 109 acute care hospitals in 10 provinces and 1 territory. Established in 1995, the program is a collaboration between:
- the Public Health Agency of Canada
- the National Microbiology Laboratory
- the Association of Medical Microbiology and Infectious Disease Canada
- sentinel hospitals across Canada
This network conducts surveillance of healthcare-associated infections on:
- surgical site and device related infections associated with: central lines hip and knee arthroplasty cerebrospinal fluid shunts adult and pediatric cardiac surgery
- hip and knee arthroplasty
- cerebrospinal fluid shunts
- adult and pediatric cardiac surgery
- antibiotic resistant organisms, such as: Candida auris Clostridioides difficile infections Carbapenemase-producing organisms Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus bloodstream infections Methicillin-susceptible Staphylococcus aureus bloodstream infections Vancomycin-resistant Enterococcus blood stream infections
- Clostridioides difficile infections
- Carbapenemase-producing organisms
- Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus bloodstream infections
- Methicillin-susceptible Staphylococcus aureus bloodstream infections
- Vancomycin-resistant Enterococcus blood stream infections
- viral respiratory infections, such as COVID-19, influenza
In addition, periodic point prevalence surveys are conducted on healthcare-associated infections including antimicrobial resistant organisms and antimicrobial usage. These surveys measure how common a particular characteristic, such as infection, illness or risk factors, during a particular point in time.
These data are used to help inform programs and policies related to:
- antimicrobial stewardship
- antimicrobial resistance
- infection prevention and control

### For more information on
- the characteristics of CNISP participating hospitals
- rates of healthcare-associated infection and antimicrobial resistant organisms
Visit our interactive data trends page for surveillance trends and recent publications
